title: Heat coefficient $a_4$ for nonminimal Laplace type operators
journal: Journal of Geometry and Physics, Vol. 141 (2019), p. 120-146
journal_ref: Journal of Geometry and Physics, Vol. 141 (2019), p. 120-146
abstract:
Given a smooth hermitean vector bundle $V$ of fiber $\mathbb{C}^N$ over a compact Riemannian manifold and $
abla$ a covariant derivative on $V$, let $P = -(\lvert g vert^{-1/2} 
abla_μ\lvert g vert^{1/2} g^{μν} u 
abla_ν+ p^μ
abla_μ+q)$ be a nonminimal Laplace type operator acting on smooth sections of $V$ where $u,\,p^ν,\,q$ are $M_N(\mathbb{C})$-valued functions with $u$ positive and invertible. For any $a \in Γ(	ext{End}(V))$, we consider the asymptotics $	ext{Tr} \,a \,e^{-tP} \sim_{t \downarrow 0} \,\sum_{r=0}^\infty a_r(a, P)\,t^{(r-d)/2}$ where the coefficients $a_r(a, P)$ can be written as an integral of the functions $a_r(a, P)(x) = 	ext{tr}\,[a(x) \,\mathcal{R}_r(x)]$.   This paper revisits the previous computation of $\mathcal{R}_2$ by the authors and is mainly devoted to a computation of $\mathcal{R}_4$. The results are presented with $u$-dependent operators which are universal (	extsl{i.e.} $P$-independent) and which act on tensor products of $u$, $p^μ$, $q$ and their derivatives via (also universal) spectral functions which are fully described.
